original_hypothesis.title: Operator Privilege Abuse: User Lock Slot Exhaustion via stakeFor
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The stakeFor function allows an operator to create locks for any user. A malicious operator can fill a user's maxLocks slots with dust amounts, preventing the user from creating their own locks.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Because maxLocks is strictly enforced (e.g., 13), an operator can call stakeFor 13 times across different epochs for a victim, consuming all their lock slots.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Operator can call _stakeFor for any account with lock_=true

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Third-party consumption of limited user resources
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: User opt-in for third-party locking or separate operator lock accounting

analysis_questions: Can the operator bypass the minLockAmount check?
How much capital does it cost the operator to grief a user?
validation_requirements: Call stakeFor as operator 13 times for a victim
Verify victim cannot call lock() themselves

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: A compromised operator bot calls stakeFor on high-value users, locking the minimum required amount 13 times. The users are now unable to lock their own large balances, losing out on the boost multiplier.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Loss of potential yield for targeted users
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Users must wait for locks to expire

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests for operator griefing of user lock limits

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Visible as many small stakeFor calls by operator

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
